**(2) (a)**

Particular | ₹
---|---
Wholesale price of Machinery | 4,00,000
(+) Handling & loading | 10,000

(+) GTA - freight directly paid | -
However, if it is arranged by Supplier but Customer enters into separate service contract, so not includible | -

(-) Price linked subsidy | (2,00,000)

**Assessable Value** | 4,26,000

---

(ii) Rooms provided to in-patient or out patients will be taxable if the room charges exceeds ₹5000/- per day. Only ICU, NICCU, NICU room charges are exempt, how here as charges exceeded the threshold GST is payable.

(iii) Generally, cosmetic surgery, plastic surgery, hair transplantation service provided will not constitute as Medical service. However if such service is provided to restore the body part, for growth development, to fight auto immune diseases, it is medical service. Hence plastic surgery conducted to repair cleft lip of new born baby is Exempt.

(iv) Ambulance services to transport critically ill patient is exempt. However if it is used for other purpose like transport of passengers it becomes taxable.

(v) Food Supply to in-patients is an exempt supply no gst will be payable.

(V) Unani, Siddha, Ayurveda, Homeopathy, Allopathy service comes under Medical services for public health and hence Homeopathic medical treatment is exempt, no GST payable.

(C)

3 (a) @ Event manage company , conducting award
function at New Delhi - place of supply
will be at location of recipient if in
India, so for award function @ New Delhi
pos = Chennai
However, award function outside India , ie,
Singapore , pos = Singapore.

5) place of supply , where event occurs in
India undertaken by unregistered person
pos = location of recipient, hence
Kerala is the place of supply.
Even if it is Malaysia , service to unregistered
person , will remain same , ie, Kerala

3 (b)
* The residential property let out for
residential purpose will be exempt for
the purpose of GST.
* The monthly rent of ₹100,000 to Mr. Smiley
will be chargeable @ RCM , as Mr. Smiley
is unregistered.
* The Tenancy premium is chargeable to
tax.

```
Q C

(6) Insurance charges (62 x 2000 x 1.10 x 1.05) 20,554
$ 2,19,2,554

Note:
1) Buying commission paid by importer is not includible
2) Unloading & handling charges at the place of importation is not includible
3) Transport charge within India are not includible

A

a) Services provided by ecommerce operators
notified u/s 9(5) includes housekeeping service,
accommodation service, transportation services &
eating food from restaurant. For these services
GST is to be paid by the ecommerce
operator.
Where the supplier is providing a facility
only through ecommerce operator and whose
turnover has not exceeded the
threshold, they are not required to get
registered, Hence MSME Put and whose turnover
is less than so law threshold & exclusively
supplying through ecommerce operator, are
not required to get registered.
```

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text:

---

a) However if it sells readymade garment through ecommerce operator, it will be required to get registered to. Supplier of goods through ecommerce operator is required as Ecom operator is not liable to pay GST in such case. However if it makes only intra-state supply & the turnover doesn't exceed threshold then it is not required to get registered.

b) The wrong availment of ITC from different heads can be modified using GST PMT-09 CGST and IGST heads can be freely transferred for the purpose of credits. However SGST credit cannot be transferred to CGST & IGST heads directly. Cross availment (transfer) is not possible in such case.

The company can deposit the Rs. 250 IGST in cash ledger & transfer it to Electronic credit ledger & then it normally avail ITC from SGST and CGST heads of Rs. 1250.

c) No, because according to the provisional assessment fee to being finalized person has paid the recovery amount accepting such order it cannot contest again upon it based on any verification report, after 6 months.

If there were discrepancies such should be addressed within six months from finalization of order, now that it has exceeded such time no redressal option.

---

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text from the image:

---

5 (b) Where any person has collected an amount as tax and not deposited such amount with government will be liable for penalty u/s 122(1) will be tax/ITC evaded/involved or 10,000 whichever is higher. Apart from that, a person being exempt from tax has collected an amount as tax is liable for imprisonment. Also, a person who is involved in ITC default, amount collected as tax will be under arrest which is cognizable & non-bailable. However such punishment would be there when tax amount had been more than 2 crores. Here, Rambumar enterprises will be liable to pay penalty u/s 122(1) i.e., Tax involved = 50,000 or 10,000 whichever is high i.e., 50,000/- is payable as penalty.

(c) Where the Goods imported has been destroyed/abandoned before clearance of home consumption, the person will be granted relief. That is he is required to pay necessary custom duty & then prove it is not for consumption as it is destroyed & then get the refund of the customs duty paid. Here Mr. Nikhil shall get refund of customs duty paid as it was destroyed before clearance of home consumption. The answer would be same as it had occurred before clearance. So he can get refund of Customs duty in such case.

---
